title: hibbertia sandifordiae
intro: Hibbertia sandifordiae, also known as the yellow peely or sandiford's guinea flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Dilleniaceae. It is native to Western Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Hibbertia sandifordiae has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant activity. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
